part2_text: Rule: transformations require validated data. In this run, was the rule satisfied before the load step?

task_text: A lab technician follows sample handling procedures.
part1_text: Steps: (1) label (2) refrigerate (3) centrifuge (4) analyse. Technician centrifuged before labelling. All pre-analysis steps correct?
part2_text: Rule: unlabelled samples must not be centrifuged. Was this rule violated?

task_text: A pharmacist checks dispensing protocol.
part1_text: Protocol: (1) verify prescription (2) check allergies (3) prepare medication (4) counsel patient. Pharmacist skipped step 2. Protocol complete?
part2_text: Rule: if allergy check is skipped, the dispensing is invalid. Based on your answer, is this dispensing valid?

task_text: A quality engineer follows testing protocols.
part1_text: Protocol: (1) calibrate (2) sample (3) test (4) log (5) review. Step 3 was done before step 2. Protocol violated?
part2_text: Rule: log only tested samples. An untested sample was logged. Rule violated?

part1_text: Agreement holds only if BOTH parties comply. One party complied; the other did not. Is the agreement in force?
part2_text: The non-compliant party claims ignorance of the terms. What social mechanism is this? A) Bad faith negotiation  B) Plausible deniability  C) Competitive bargaining  D) Escalation

part1_text: Policy: confidential information must not be shared outside the team. A team member shares it with an adjacent department. Policy violated?
part2_text: After this violation, team trust declines. What is the most likely driver? A) The team is oversensitive  B) Breach of confidentiality damages psychological safety  C) Rules are too strict  D) Information sharing is positive

task_text: A policy analyst checks stakeholder thresholds and equity implications.

part2_text: The policy disproportionately affects a minority group. What ethical principle demands particular scrutiny? A) Utility maximisation  B) Procedural justice  C) Equity and fairness  D) Cost efficiency

part1_text: All decisions require unanimous consensus. 4 of 5 members agree. Does the decision pass?

part1_text: Project kickoff: Monday Feb 3. Milestone A in 2 weeks. Milestone B: 3 weeks after A. Milestone B date?
part2_text: Rule: if Milestone B is before March 14, proceed to Phase 2. Based on your answer, proceed?

task_text: A nurse checks medication timing.
part1_text: First dose 6:00 AM. Medication every 8 hours. Time of fourth dose?
part2_text: Rule: no dose after 10:00 PM. Is the fourth dose permissible?

task_text: A supervisor handles a bystander situation and follows incident reporting procedures.
part1_text: An employee witnesses workplace harassment but says nothing fearing retaliation. What social phenomenon explains this? A) Bystander effect  B) Social loafing  C) Diffusion of responsibility  D) Both A and C
part2_text: Reporting procedure: (1) document incident (2) report to supervisor (3) HR review (4) investigation (5) outcome communication. Supervisor launches investigation before HR review. Protocol followed?

task_text: A manager gives performance feedback and follows the feedback procedure.
part1_text: A manager gives positive feedback only when others are watching but gives negative feedback in private. What behaviour does this represent? A) Inconsistent feedback  B) Public relations management  C) Two-faced or performative behaviour  D) Situational leadership
part2_text: Feedback protocol: (1) prepare notes (2) schedule session (3) deliver feedback (4) agree on action items (5) follow up. Manager delivers feedback without scheduling a formal session. Protocol followed?

task_text: A facilitator runs a brainstorming workshop and follows group process steps.
part1_text: Participants in a brainstorming session start critiquing ideas as soon as they are raised. What brainstorming norm is being violated?
part2_text: Workshop procedure: (1) set ground rules (2) warm-up (3) brainstorm (4) prioritise (5) action planning. Group jumps from brainstorming directly to action planning. Protocol followed?

task_text: An HR manager handles a complaint impartially and follows resolution procedures.
part1_text: An employee raises a complaint about a colleague. Manager publicly sides with the senior colleague before investigating. What professional principle is violated?
part2_text: Complaint resolution: (1) receive complaint (2) acknowledge receipt (3) investigate (4) communicate findings (5) implement resolution. Manager skips investigation and implements resolution directly. Protocol followed?

answer_b: maximize product exposure and encourage impulse purchases
